title: WOODS Time-Series OOD Generalization Benchmarks

data_urls.access_notes: Use to recover official dataset download and benchmark commands.

instructions: Use the WOODS framework to select one benchmark dataset and inspect its upstream access requirements.
Record dataset name, modality, domain labels, split, sequence length, sampling rate, and labels.
Use official domain splits as operating points, then construct SPECTRA curves using temporal/subject/device/domain support.

scale.download_size: variable across ten time-series benchmarks; selected dataset first

recommended_axes.name: domain_support
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity of WOODS domain labels such as subject, device, source, environment, or temporal domain.

recommended_axes.name: temporal_shape_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Dynamic time warping, shapelet, embedding, or frequency-feature similarity to training series.

recommended_axes.name: sensor_or_subject_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Support over subject, device, channel, activity, or acquisition metadata when available.

useful_for: Broad time-series OOD SPECTRA audits beyond UCR/UEA shape-only examples.
Testing domain-generalization algorithms on sequential data.
Exploring whether official WOODS domains are points on a continuous signal-support curve.
leakage_risks: Subject or recording-session leakage can inflate performance.
Domain labels can encode the benchmark split and should be treated as operating points.
Some component datasets may have separate licenses or access constraints.

paperclip_provenance.notes: Paperclip identified WOODS as a time-series OOD benchmark; official site lists ten sequential benchmarks and framework/documentation links.
